message:
beads: record fcp-store/fcp-mesh triage findings and status updates from the 2026-04-20 cross-crate review

Single-file JSONL update to `.beads/issues.jsonl`. Eight issue
records rewritten (6 new issues, 2 existing issues updated with
status transitions). No database rebuild required -- the file is
the source of truth and a downstream `br sync --flush-only` will
rebuild the DB snapshot.

New issues recorded
-------------------

* `flywheel_connectors-4g0qr` -- **fcp-store: WAL/snapshot replay
  accepts attacker-chosen object_id without verifying canonical
  content hash.** The `StoredObject::canonical_bytes` invariant
  says `object_id == canonical_hash("FCP2-OBJECT-V1" ||
  canonical_cbor(header) || body)`. Neither `MemoryObjectStore::put`
  nor `DurableObjectState::insert_loaded` (called from snapshot
  recovery and WAL replay) recomputes the ID. A write-local
  attacker with WAL access can forge `object_id -> wrong body`
  pairings without breaking any signature. Axiom-7 / Axiom-9 lens.

* `flywheel_connectors-g27rl` -- **fcp-mesh/node: planner zones
  never populated; zone-policy filtering broken in production.**
  Upstream of the already-closed fqzmp.

* `flywheel_connectors-opoux` -- **fcp-mesh/node: zone-authorization
  gate silently bypassed for unknown peers and peers without
  populated zone state.** Closed by 60b57218 (2026-04-20); recorded
  here for history.

* `flywheel_connectors-q92i7` -- **fcp-mesh/node:
  handle_symbol_request accepts caller-supplied is_authenticated,
  OR'd with local state.** Peer rate limits are trivially bypassable
  by setting the bit in an incoming request.

* `flywheel_connectors-sfunf` -- **fcp-store/object_store:
  MemoryObjectStore::put quota TOCTOU.** The `drop(objects)`
  followed by `used_bytes.fetch_add` opens a window where two
  concurrent writers can each pass the quota check and then both
  admit. Closed via 66e52773 + 1035d54b regression test.

* `flywheel_connectors-yhmwv` -- **fcp-store/durable: WAL recovery
  uses unbounded read_until.** Torn or oversized single-record
  replay allocates without bound. Closed via 4cea6816.

Existing issue status updates
-----------------------------

* `flywheel_connectors-fqzmp` -- "planner zones never populated"
  (prior JSON entry rewritten). Status flipped to reflect that
  a75b9e9d (`fcp-mesh/node: thread local and peer zones through
  build_planner_input`) is the fix.

* `flywheel_connectors-kzrfp` -- "IbltPlaceholder in gossip path"
  (prior JSON entry rewritten). Status flipped to reflect the
  two-step fix: d69761b7 migrated the type and dfe0354a migrated
  the on-wire format to CBOR Iblt.

Why this is committed separately
--------------------------------
Triage/tracking data, not code. Committing it as its own log
entry keeps the code-change commits focused on the actual fix
diffs and lets a reviewer read the beads update as a single
coherent "what we learned and decided this review round" summary.

Diff size
---------
+8 / -2 lines in `.beads/issues.jsonl` -- the 2 replaced lines are
full JSON records (JSONL is one record per line) for the two
existing issues being updated; the 8 added are 6 new records plus
the 2 rewritten entries with updated status/close fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
